sourceTitle: How teams can use comments for better collaboration

questions.question: What is the primary difference between page comments and block comments in Notion?

options.text: Page comments are visible to anyone, while block comments are private.
options.explanation: Both comment types are visible to anyone with access to the page; privacy is not the distinguishing factor.

options.text: Page comments apply to the entire page, whereas block comments are attached to a specific block such as a paragraph or image.
options.explanation: Correct: page comments are for high‑level feedback about the whole page, while block comments target individual blocks.

options.text: Block comments can only be added by admins, while page comments can be added by any team member.
options.explanation: Permission levels are the same for both; any collaborator can add either type of comment.

options.text: Page comments automatically resolve after 24 hours, but block comments do not.
options.explanation: Resolution is manual for both comment types; there is no automatic timer.

questions.question: Which of the following actions can you perform by clicking the three‑dot (•••) icon on a comment?

options.text: Pin the comment to the top of the page.
options.explanation: Notion does not have a pin‑to‑top feature for comments.

options.text: Edit, delete, or resolve the comment.
options.explanation: The three‑dot menu provides options to edit, delete, or resolve a comment.

options.text: Convert the comment into a separate Notion page.
options.explanation: Comments cannot be turned into standalone pages directly.

options.text: Assign a due date to the comment.
options.explanation: Due dates are added using the @date syntax, not via the three‑dot menu.

questions.question: If you tag a teammate in a comment on a private page they haven't been invited to, what will happen?

options.text: They will receive a notification but be unable to view the comment.
options.explanation: Notion prevents the notification entirely if the person lacks access.

options.text: They will not receive a notification until you share the page with them.
options.explanation: The article states that a user must have access to the page before they can be notified of a tag.

options.text: The tag will be automatically removed from the comment.
options.explanation: The tag stays, but the notification is suppressed until access is granted.

options.text: Notion will create a copy of the page for the tagged user.
options.explanation: Notion does not duplicate pages for mentioned users.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a recommended best practice for managing comment discussions?

options.text: Outline the review process and specify required stakeholders.
options.explanation: This is explicitly recommended in the guide.

options.text: Set a clear deadline for feedback within the comment.
options.explanation: Including a deadline is a suggested practice.

options.text: Leave comments without tagging anyone to keep the thread open.
options.explanation: The guide encourages tagging the right people; leaving untagged comments can lead to missed feedback.

options.text: Guide conversations by noting what reviewers should focus on.
options.explanation: Guiding reviewers is part of the best‑practice advice.

questions.question: How can you add a specific date or deadline to a comment using Notion’s @ syntax?

options.text: Type @ followed by the date in MM/DD format, e.g., @12/01.
options.explanation: The format requires month name or numeric month with slash, but the guide shows @1/12 resolves to Jan 12, 2020.

options.text: Type @ and then select a date from the calendar that appears.
options.explanation: Typing @ alone does not open a calendar; you must type a recognizable date string.

options.text: Type @ followed by the month/day (or month/year) like @1/12, then click the generated date to customize.
options.explanation: Correct: @1/12 creates a timestamp that can be edited for time, range, and timezone.

options.text: Use @deadline as a shortcut that automatically adds today’s date.
options.explanation: There is no @deadline shortcut in Notion.

questions.question: When would it be most appropriate to use a block comment instead of a page comment?

options.text: When you want to give overall feedback about the entire project's timeline.
options.explanation: Overall project feedback belongs in a page comment.

options.text: When you need to discuss a specific paragraph, image, or code snippet within the page.
options.explanation: Block comments target individual blocks for granular feedback.

options.text: When you are assigning a task to a teammate that spans multiple pages.
options.explanation: Task assignment across pages is better handled with a page comment or a separate task database.

options.text: When you want to reference another Notion page in your comment.
options.explanation: Referencing another page can be done in either comment type; the decision isn’t about referencing.

questions.question: A team wants to ensure that every comment receives a response within 48 hours, but they also need a way to track overdue comments without manually checking each one. Which combination of Notion features best supports this requirement?

options.text: Use page comments with @date tags for the deadline and enable email notifications for comment activity.
options.explanation: Page comments let you set a deadline via @date, and notifications remind assignees, providing automated tracking of overdue items.

options.text: Add block comments with @mentions only, then rely on the sidebar reminder feature.
options.explanation: Block comments alone don’t provide a built‑in deadline; the sidebar reminder only triggers after a comment is resolved, not for overdue tracking.

options.text: Create a separate database to log each comment and use a formula to calculate overdue status.
options.explanation: While possible, this adds unnecessary complexity compared to the built‑in deadline and notification system.

options.text: Turn on push notifications and manually check the comment timestamp each day.
options.explanation: Manual checking defeats the purpose of automated tracking; push notifications alone won’t flag overdue comments.

questions.question: Why is it important to establish a "window for input" in a top‑of‑page comment, and how does Notion help enforce this window?

options.text: It prevents teammates from commenting after the deadline, and Notion automatically locks the page.
options.explanation: Notion does not lock pages; comments can still be added after the deadline.

options.text: It clarifies expectations and triggers a reminder notification if the comment isn’t resolved by the specified date.
options.explanation: The guide states that unresolved comments generate a reminder, helping enforce the feedback window.

options.text: It automatically assigns the task to the next person in line, ensuring continuous workflow.
options.explanation: Automatic task reassignment isn’t a feature of comment windows.

options.text: It converts the comment into a calendar event that blocks team members’ schedules.
options.explanation: Comments do not become calendar events; they remain comment threads.
